title: gilia cana
intro: Gilia cana, also known as the white gilia or white gilia flower, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The herbal preparations of Gilia cana include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
